questions.text: If A @ B means A is the son of B, A # B means A is the brother of B, and if A * B means A is the mother of B, then what does X # Y @ Z * W mean, if Z has 2 sons and one daughter?
questions.questionText: If A @ B means A is the son of B, A # B means A is the brother of B, and if A * B means A is the mother of B, then what does X # Y @ Z * W mean, if Z has 2 sons and one daughter?
questions.question: If A @ B means A is the son of B, A # B means A is the brother of B, and if A * B means A is the mother of B, then what does X # Y @ Z * W mean, if Z has 2 sons and one daughter?
questions.prompt: If A @ B means A is the son of B, A # B means A is the brother of B, and if A * B means A is the mother of B, then what does X # Y @ Z * W mean, if Z has 2 sons and one daughter?
questions.html: If A @ B means A is the son of B, A # B means A is the brother of B, and if A * B means A is the mother of B, then what does X # Y @ Z * W mean, if Z has 2 sons and one daughter?
questions.questionHtml: If A @ B means A is the son of B, A # B means A is the brother of B, and if A * B means A is the mother of B, then what does X # Y @ Z * W mean, if Z has 2 sons and one daughter?
questions.solutionText: Solution Decoding the symbols: @ means "is the son of" # means "is the brother of" ∗ means "is the mother of" Expression: X # Y @ Z ∗ W Reading step by step: Z ∗ W ⇒ Z is the mother of W Y @ Z ⇒ Y is the son of Z X # Y ⇒ X is the brother of Y So: Z (mother) has children Y (son), X (brother of Y , so also son of Z ), and W . Given Z has 2 sons and 1 daughter ⇒ X and Y are the 2 sons, and W is the daughter. W is the sister of X .
questions.solution: Solution Decoding the symbols: @ means "is the son of" # means "is the brother of" ∗ means "is the mother of" Expression: X # Y @ Z ∗ W Reading step by step: Z ∗ W ⇒ Z is the mother of W Y @ Z ⇒ Y is the son of Z X # Y ⇒ X is the brother of Y So: Z (mother) has children Y (son), X (brother of Y , so also son of Z ), and W . Given Z has 2 sons and 1 daughter ⇒ X and Y are the 2 sons, and W is the daughter. W is the sister of X .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Decoding the symbols:
@ means "is the son of"
# means "is the brother of"
∗ means "is the mother of"

Expression: X#Y@Z∗W

Reading step by step:
Z∗W ⇒ Z is the mother of W
Y@Z ⇒ Y is the son of Z
X#Y ⇒ X is the brother of Y

So: Z (mother) has children Y (son), X (brother of Y, so also son of Z), and W.

Given Z has 2 sons and 1 daughter ⇒ X and Y are the 2 sons, and W is the daughter.

W is the sister of X.

questions.text: A cube has its six faces painted as follows: Top - Red, Bottom - Blue, Front - Green, Back - Yellow, Left - White, Right - Black. The cube is cut into 216 smaller, equal cubes ( 6 × 6 × 6 ). How many of the small cubes will have exactly two faces painted?
questions.questionText: A cube has its six faces painted as follows: Top - Red, Bottom - Blue, Front - Green, Back - Yellow, Left - White, Right - Black. The cube is cut into 216 smaller, equal cubes ( 6 × 6 × 6 ). How many of the small cubes will have exactly two faces painted?
questions.question: A cube has its six faces painted as follows: Top - Red, Bottom - Blue, Front - Green, Back - Yellow, Left - White, Right - Black. The cube is cut into 216 smaller, equal cubes ( 6 × 6 × 6 ). How many of the small cubes will have exactly two faces painted?
questions.prompt: A cube has its six faces painted as follows: Top - Red, Bottom - Blue, Front - Green, Back - Yellow, Left - White, Right - Black. The cube is cut into 216 smaller, equal cubes ( 6 × 6 × 6 ). How many of the small cubes will have exactly two faces painted?
questions.html:
A cube has its six faces painted as follows:
Top - Red, Bottom - Blue, Front - Green, Back - Yellow, Left - White, Right - Black.
The cube is cut into 216 smaller, equal cubes (6×6×6).
How many of the small cubes will have exactly two faces painted?
questions.questionHtml:
A cube has its six faces painted as follows:
Top - Red, Bottom - Blue, Front - Green, Back - Yellow, Left - White, Right - Black.
The cube is cut into 216 smaller, equal cubes (6×6×6).
How many of the small cubes will have exactly two faces painted?
questions.solutionText: Solution When a cube is cut into 6 × 6 × 6 = 216 smaller cubes, the small cubes with exactly two faces painted are the edge cubes (excluding corners). A cube has 12 edges. Each edge of a 6 × 6 × 6 cube has 6 − 2 = 4 small cubes with exactly two painted faces (excluding the 2 corner cubes on each edge). Total = 12 × 4 = 48
questions.solution: Solution When a cube is cut into 6 × 6 × 6 = 216 smaller cubes, the small cubes with exactly two faces painted are the edge cubes (excluding corners). A cube has 12 edges. Each edge of a 6 × 6 × 6 cube has 6 − 2 = 4 small cubes with exactly two painted faces (excluding the 2 corner cubes on each edge). Total = 12 × 4 = 48
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
When a cube is cut into 6×6×6=216 smaller cubes, the small cubes with exactly two faces painted are the edge cubes (excluding corners).

A cube has 12 edges. Each edge of a 6×6×6 cube has 6−2=4 small cubes with exactly two painted faces (excluding the 2 corner cubes on each edge).

Total =12×4=48

questions.questionHtml:
Statement: "Deliver this encrypted hard drive directly to the client's regional director before 5:00 PM today, and under no circumstances should you hand it over to their standard mailroom staff," the firm's senior partner instructed her associate.

Assumptions:
I. The associate has a viable way to physically reach the client's regional director and bypass the standard mailroom before the 5:00 PM deadline.
II. The client's standard mailroom staff cannot be trusted with confidential or encrypted data.
questions.solutionText: Solution Assumption I is implicit: For the senior partner to issue this specific, time-sensitive command with strict constraints, she must inherently assume that the associate is capable of executing it. If she assumed the associate had no way to bypass the mailroom or couldn't make it by 5:00 PM, she would not have given the instruction in this manner. It is a necessary prerequisite for the command. Assumption II is NOT implicit: While it is highly tempting to assume the mailroom staff is "untrustworthy" (because why else would she bypass them?), this is a logical leap. The partner's instruction could be based on a strict legal protocol, a desire for premium "white-glove" customer service, or the need for a direct signature from the director. We cannot definitively assume she believes the mailroom staff will steal or mishandle the drive; we only know she wants them bypassed.

questions.text: Find the odd one out.
questions.questionText: Find the odd one out.
questions.question: Find the odd one out.
questions.prompt: Find the odd one out.
questions.html: Find the odd one out.
questions.questionHtml: Find the odd one out.
questions.solutionText: Solution In all other pairs, the sum of the digits of the second number is twice the sum of the digits of the first number. 1. 12 − 42 → 1 + 2 = 3 × 2 = 6 = 4 + 2 2. 14 − 82 → 1 + 4 = 5 × 2 = 10 = 8 + 2 3. 23 − 64 → 2 + 3 = 5 × 2 = 10 = 6 + 4 4. 36 − 72 → 3 + 6 = 9 × 2 = 18 ≠ 7 + 2 Hence, ' 36 − 72 ' is the odd one out.
questions.solution: Solution In all other pairs, the sum of the digits of the second number is twice the sum of the digits of the first number. 1. 12 − 42 → 1 + 2 = 3 × 2 = 6 = 4 + 2 2. 14 − 82 → 1 + 4 = 5 × 2 = 10 = 8 + 2 3. 23 − 64 → 2 + 3 = 5 × 2 = 10 = 6 + 4 4. 36 − 72 → 3 + 6 = 9 × 2 = 18 ≠ 7 + 2 Hence, ' 36 − 72 ' is the odd one out.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
In all other pairs, the sum of the digits of the second number is twice the sum of the digits of the first number.
1. 12−42→1+2=3×2=6=4+2
2. 14−82→1+4=5×2=10=8+2
3. 23−64→2+3=5×2=10=6+4
4. 36−72→3+6=9×2=18≠7+2
Hence, '36−72' is the odd one out.

questions.text: In the following question, there are some statements followed by some conclusions. questions.questionHtml:
In the following question, there are some statements followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows/follow from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts.

Statements:
All clerks are professors.
No professor is a doctor.
All doctors are managers.

Conclusions:
I. No manager is a clerk.
II. All professors being managers is a possibility.
questions.solutionText: Solution In conclusion II we need to derive a relationship between the classes "manager" and "clerk" which are present in S3 and S1 respectively. By applying the deduction method on S1 and S2, we get "No clerk is a doctor". Now taking this and S3, we can once again apply the deduction method and get the conclusion as "Some managers are not clerks" which is not given as a conclusion. Conclusion I hence doesn't follow. For conclusion II, we can take S2 and S3 and apply the deduction method. The derived conclusion is "Some managers are not professors". Here, we are not sure of the elements of the class "professors" and therefore the possibility "All professors being managers" exists. Conclusion II, thus, follows.
questions.solution: Solution In conclusion II we need to derive a relationship between the classes "manager" and "clerk" which are present in S3 and S1 respectively. By applying the deduction method on S1 and S2, we get "No clerk is a doctor". Now taking this and S3, we can once again apply the deduction method and get the conclusion as "Some managers are not clerks" which is not given as a conclusion. Conclusion I hence doesn't follow. For conclusion II, we can take S2 and S3 and apply the deduction method. The derived conclusion is "Some managers are not professors". Here, we are not sure of the elements of the class "professors" and therefore the possibility "All professors being managers" exists. Conclusion II, thus, follows.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution


In conclusion II we need to derive a relationship between the classes "manager" and "clerk" which are present in S3 and S1 respectively.

By applying the deduction method on S1 and S2, we get "No clerk is a doctor". Now taking this and S3, we can once again apply the deduction method and get the conclusion as "Some managers are not clerks" which is not given as a conclusion. Conclusion I hence doesn't follow.

For conclusion II, we can take S2 and S3 and apply the deduction method. The derived conclusion is "Some managers are not professors". Here, we are not sure of the elements of the class "professors" and therefore the possibility "All professors being managers" exists. Conclusion II, thus, follows.

questions.subject: English & Logical Reasoning
questions.question: If 4 − 2 − 3 = 33 and 5 − 3 − 1 = 24 and 2 − 6 − 2 = 34 . Then 6 − 4 − 2 = ?
questions.html: If 4−2−3=33 and 5−3−1=24 and 2−6−2=34. Then 6−4−2=?
questions.questionHtml: If 4−2−3=33 and 5−3−1=24 and 2−6−2=34. Then 6−4−2=?
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Pattern: a−b−c=a×b×c+(a+b+c)

4−2−3=4×2×3+(4+2+3)=24+9=33
5−3−1=5×3×1+(5+3+1)=15+9=24
2−6−2=2×6×2+(2+6+2)=24+10=34

6−4−2=6×4×2+(6+4+2)=48+12=60

Answer: Option (1): 60

questions.subject: English & Logical Reasoning
questions.html: 6, 11, 24, 49, 90, ?
questions.questionHtml: 6, 11, 24, 49, 90, ?
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Given series is, 6, 11, 24, 49, 90, ?

So, the method used in this series is: +12+22,, +22+32, +32+42, +42+52.......

11 = 6 + 12 +22
24 = 11 + 22 + 32
49 = 24 +32 +42
90 = 49 +4² +52
? = 90 + 52 + 62 = 151.

Hence, the answer is 151.

questions.questionHtml: If STRANGE is coded as 72, EFFECT is coded as 25, and DREAM is coded as 29, then VITAMIN is coded as _______.
questions.solutionText: Solution The logic is: (Sum of the numerical values of the consonants) - (Sum of the numerical values of the vowels) The code for VITAMIN using the below mentioned logic is: (V+T+M+N) - (I+A+I) (22+20+13+14) - (9+1+9) 69 - 19 = 50
questions.solution: Solution The logic is: (Sum of the numerical values of the consonants) - (Sum of the numerical values of the vowels) The code for VITAMIN using the below mentioned logic is: (V+T+M+N) - (I+A+I) (22+20+13+14) - (9+1+9) 69 - 19 = 50
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The logic is: (Sum of the numerical values of the consonants) - (Sum of the numerical values of the vowels)

The code for VITAMIN using the below mentioned logic is:
(V+T+M+N) - (I+A+I)
(22+20+13+14) - (9+1+9)
69 - 19 = 50

questions.solutionText: Solution Given Boolean expression: ∼ ( p ∨ q ) ∨ ( ∼ p ∧ q ) Step 1: Apply De Morgan's Law: ∼ ( p ∨ q ) =∼ p ∧ ∼ q So expression becomes: ( ∼ p ∧ ∼ q ) ∨ ( ∼ p ∧ q ) Step 2: Distribute ∼ p : ∼ p ∧ ( ∼ q ∨ q ) ( ∼ q ∨ q ) = True ⇒∼ p ∧ True =∼ p Final Answer: (d) ~p
questions.solution: Solution Given Boolean expression: ∼ ( p ∨ q ) ∨ ( ∼ p ∧ q ) Step 1: Apply De Morgan's Law: ∼ ( p ∨ q ) =∼ p ∧ ∼ q So expression becomes: ( ∼ p ∧ ∼ q ) ∨ ( ∼ p ∧ q ) Step 2: Distribute ∼ p : ∼ p ∧ ( ∼ q ∨ q ) ( ∼ q ∨ q ) = True ⇒∼ p ∧ True =∼ p Final Answer: (d) ~p

Given Boolean expression:
∼(p∨q)∨(∼p∧q)

∼(p∨q)=∼p∧∼q
So expression becomes:
(∼p∧∼q)∨(∼p∧q)
Step 2: Distribute ∼p :
∼p∧(∼q∨q)(∼q∨q)= True ⇒∼p∧ True =∼p
Final Answer: (d) ~p

questions.html: cot-1(cosα)-tan-1(cosα)=x then sinx is equal to

questions.solutionText: Solution Given, cot - 1 cos α - tan - 1 cos α = x ∵ cot - 1 x = tan - 1 1 x , ∀ x > 0 ∴ tan - 1 1 cos α - tan - 1 cos α = x We know tan - 1 A - tan - 1 B = tan - 1 A - B 1 + A B ⇒ tan - 1 1 cos α - cos α 1 + 1 cos α . cos α = x ⇒ tan - 1 1 - cos α 2 cos α = x ⇒ tan x = 1 - cos α 2 cos α We know sec 2 x = 1 + tan 2 x ⇒ sec 2 x = 1 + 1 - cos α 2 cos α 2 ⇒ sec 2 x = 4 cos α + ( 1 - cos α ) 4 cos α 2 ⇒ sec 2 x = ( 1 + cos α ) 4 cos α 2 Now, sin 2 x = tan 2 x sec 2 x ⇒ sin 2 x = 1 - cos α 2 cos α 2 ( 1 + cos α ) 2 cos α 2 ⇒ sin x = 1 - cos α 1 + cos α ⇒ sin x = 2 sin 2 α 2 2 cos 2 α 2 ⇒ sin x = tan 2 α 2
questions.solution: Solution Given, cot - 1 cos α - tan - 1 cos α = x ∵ cot - 1 x = tan - 1 1 x , ∀ x > 0 ∴ tan - 1 1 cos α - tan - 1 cos α = x We know tan - 1 A - tan - 1 B = tan - 1 A - B 1 + A B ⇒ tan - 1 1 cos α - cos α 1 + 1 cos α . cos α = x ⇒ tan - 1 1 - cos α 2 cos α = x ⇒ tan x = 1 - cos α 2 cos α We know sec 2 x = 1 + tan 2 x ⇒ sec 2 x = 1 + 1 - cos α 2 cos α 2 ⇒ sec 2 x = 4 cos α + ( 1 - cos α ) 4 cos α 2 ⇒ sec 2 x = ( 1 + cos α ) 4 cos α 2 Now, sin 2 x = tan 2 x sec 2 x ⇒ sin 2 x = 1 - cos α 2 cos α 2 ( 1 + cos α ) 2 cos α 2 ⇒ sin x = 1 - cos α 1 + cos α ⇒ sin x = 2 sin 2 α 2 2 cos 2 α 2 ⇒ sin x = tan 2 α 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Given, cot-1cosα-tan-1cosα=x

∵ cot-1x=tan-11x ,∀ x >0

∴ tan-11cosα-tan-1cosα=x

We know tan-1A-tan-1B=tan-1A-B1+AB

⇒ tan-11cosα-cosα1+1cosα.cosα=x

⇒tan-11-cosα2cosα=x

⇒tanx=1-cosα2cosα

We know sec2x=1+tan2x

⇒sec2x=1+1-cosα2cosα2

⇒sec2x=4cosα+(1-cosα)4cosα2

⇒sec2x=(1+cosα)4cosα2

Now, sin2x=tan2xsec2x

⇒sin2x =1-cosα2cosα2(1+cosα)2cosα2

⇒sinx=1-cosα1+cosα

⇒sinx=2sin2α22cos2α2

⇒sinx=tan2α2

questions.text: Let A be the set of all 3 × 3 symmetric matrices all of whose entries are either 0 or 1 , five of these entries are 1 and four of them are zero. Then the number of matrices in A is
questions.questionText: Let A be the set of all 3 × 3 symmetric matrices all of whose entries are either 0 or 1 , five of these entries are 1 and four of them are zero. Then the number of matrices in A is
questions.question: Let A be the set of all 3 × 3 symmetric matrices all of whose entries are either 0 or 1 , five of these entries are 1 and four of them are zero. Then the number of matrices in A is
questions.prompt: Let A be the set of all 3 × 3 symmetric matrices all of whose entries are either 0 or 1 , five of these entries are 1 and four of them are zero. Then the number of matrices in A is
questions.html: Let A be the set of all 3×3 symmetric matrices all of whose entries are either 0 or 1, five of these entries are 1 and four of them are zero. Then the number of matrices in A is
questions.questionHtml: Let A be the set of all 3×3 symmetric matrices all of whose entries are either 0 or 1, five of these entries are 1 and four of them are zero. Then the number of matrices in A is
questions.solutionText: Solution Matrices are symmetric. So, we can only arrange entries of either upper right portion of matrices or lower left portion of matrices. Assume we are arranging lower left portion and diagonal. So, two cases arise. Case 1 : When non-diagonal elements have 2 zeros and 4 ones and diagonal elements have 2 zeros and 1 one ∴ Number of ways = 3 ! 2 ! × 1 × 3 ! 2 ! = 9 (arrangement of non-diagonal followed by diagonal) Case 2 : When non-diagonal elements has 4 zeros and 2 ones and diagonal elements have 3 ones ∴ Number of ways = 3 ! 2 ! × 1 × 3 ! 3 ! = 3 (arrangement of non-diagonal followed by diagonal) Hence, number of matrices in A = 9 + 3 = 12
questions.solution: Solution Matrices are symmetric. So, we can only arrange entries of either upper right portion of matrices or lower left portion of matrices. Assume we are arranging lower left portion and diagonal. So, two cases arise. Case 1 : When non-diagonal elements have 2 zeros and 4 ones and diagonal elements have 2 zeros and 1 one ∴ Number of ways = 3 ! 2 ! × 1 × 3 ! 2 ! = 9 (arrangement of non-diagonal followed by diagonal) Case 2 : When non-diagonal elements has 4 zeros and 2 ones and diagonal elements have 3 ones ∴ Number of ways = 3 ! 2 ! × 1 × 3 ! 3 ! = 3 (arrangement of non-diagonal followed by diagonal) Hence, number of matrices in A = 9 + 3 = 12
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Matrices are symmetric. So, we can only arrange entries of either upper right portion of matrices or lower left portion of matrices.

Assume we are arranging lower left portion and diagonal. So, two cases arise.

Case1: When non-diagonal elements have 2 zeros and 4 ones and diagonal elements have 2 zeros and 1 one

∴ Number of ways = 3!2!×1×3!2!=9 (arrangement of non-diagonal followed by diagonal)

Case 2: When non-diagonal elements has 4 zeros and 2 ones and diagonal elements have 3 ones

∴ Number of ways = 3!2!×1×3!3!=3 (arrangement of non-diagonal followed by diagonal)

Hence, number of matrices in A=9+3=12

questions.text: The expression tan x - π 2 . cos 3 π 2 + x - sin 3 7 π 2 - x cos x - π 2 . tan 3 π 2 + x simplifies to:

questions.questionHtml: The expression tanx-π2.cos3π2+x-sin37π2-x cosx-π2.tan3π2+x simplifies to:
questions.solutionText: Solution Let, A = tan x - π 2 . cos 3 π 2 + x - sin 3 7 π 2 - x cos x - π 2 . tan 3 π 2 + x ∵ tan x - π 2 = - cot x ; cos 3 π 2 + x = sin x ; sin 3 7 π 2 - x = cos x ; cos x - π 2 = sin x ; tan 3 π 2 + x = - cotx Hence, A = - cot x sin x + cos 3 x sin x . ( - cot x ) = - cos x sin x sinx + cos 3 x sin x . - cos x sin x ⇒ A = - cos x + cos 3 x - cos x ⇒ A = 1 - cos 2 x ⇒ A = sin 2 x Hence, this is the required solution.
questions.solution: Solution Let, A = tan x - π 2 . cos 3 π 2 + x - sin 3 7 π 2 - x cos x - π 2 . tan 3 π 2 + x ∵ tan x - π 2 = - cot x ; cos 3 π 2 + x = sin x ; sin 3 7 π 2 - x = cos x ; cos x - π 2 = sin x ; tan 3 π 2 + x = - cotx Hence, A = - cot x sin x + cos 3 x sin x . ( - cot x ) = - cos x sin x sinx + cos 3 x sin x . - cos x sin x ⇒ A = - cos x + cos 3 x - cos x ⇒ A = 1 - cos 2 x ⇒ A = sin 2 x Hence, this is the required solution.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Let, A=tanx-π2.cos3π2+x-sin37π2-xcosx-π2.tan3π2+x

∵ tanx-π2=-cotx; cos3π2+x=sinx; sin37π2-x=cosx; cosx-π2=sinx; tan3π2+x=-cotx

Hence, A= -cotxsinx+cos3xsinx.(-cotx)=-cosxsinxsinx+cos3xsinx.-cosxsinx

⇒A=-cosx+cos3x-cosx

⇒A=1-cos2x

⇒A=sin2x

Hence, this is the required solution.

questions.solutionText: Solution Draw BS parallel to DP as shown Let A P = P B = x ⇒ D S = x ⇒ S C = x △ B A Q ∼ △ P A R ∴ AB AP = AQ AR ⇒ 2 x x = AQ AR ⇒ AQ = 2 AR Thus R is mid point of AQ . i.e. AR = RQ Similarly Δ CQS Δ CRD ∴ CQ = RQ Thus we get AR = RQ = CQ Hence point R divides A C in the ratio 1 : 2

Hence point R divides AC in the ratio 1:2

questions.solutionText: Solution The given circle is x 2 + y 2 − 2 x = 0 . Let ( x 1 , y 1 ) be the middle point of any chord of this circle, than its equation is S 1 = T . or x 1 2 + y 1 2 − 2 x 1 = x x 1 + y y 1 − ( x + x 1 ) If it passes through ( 0 , 0 ) , then x 1 2 + y 1 2 − 2 x 1 = − x 1 ⇒ x 1 2 + y 1 2 − x 1 = 0 Hence the required locus of the given point ( x 1 , y 1 ) is x 2 + y 2 − x = 0 .

The given circle is x2+y2−2x=0. Let (x1,y1) be the middle point of any chord of this circle, than its equation is S1=T.
or x12+y12−2x1=xx1+yy1−(x+x1)

x12+y12−2x1=−x1⇒x12+y12−x1=0

questions.solutionText: Solution We have statements p , q → T and r , s → F Option (a) q ∧ r ∨ ∼ p ∧ s ≡ T ∧ F ∨ F ∧ F ≡ F ∨ F ≡ F Option (b) ∼ p → q ↔ r ∧ s ≡ p ∨ q → r ∨ s ∵ p → q ≡ ∼ p ∨ q ≡ ∼ p v q ∨ r ∧ s ∧ p ∨ q ∨ ∼ r ∧ s ∵ p ↔ q ≡ ∼ p ∨ q ∧ p v - q ≡ ∼ T ∨ T ∨ F ∧ F ∧ T ∨ F ∨ F ∧ F ≡ F ∨ F ∧ T ∨ T ≡ F ∧ T ≡ F Option (c) p → q ∨ r ↔ s ≡ ∼ p ∨ q ∨ ∼ r ∨ s ∧ r ∨ ∼ s ∵ p → q ≡ ∼ p ∨ q ≡ F ∨ T ∨ T ∨ F ∧ F ∨ T ≡ T ∨ T ∧ T ≡ T ∨ T ≡ T Option (d) do similar as option (a).
questions.solution: Solution We have statements p , q → T and r , s → F Option (a) q ∧ r ∨ ∼ p ∧ s ≡ T ∧ F ∨ F ∧ F ≡ F ∨ F ≡ F Option (b) ∼ p → q ↔ r ∧ s ≡ p ∨ q → r ∨ s ∵ p → q ≡ ∼ p ∨ q ≡ ∼ p v q ∨ r ∧ s ∧ p ∨ q ∨ ∼ r ∧ s ∵ p ↔ q ≡ ∼ p ∨ q ∧ p v - q ≡ ∼ T ∨ T ∨ F ∧ F ∧ T ∨ F ∨ F ∧ F ≡ F ∨ F ∧ T ∨ T ≡ F ∧ T ≡ F Option (c) p → q ∨ r ↔ s ≡ ∼ p ∨ q ∨ ∼ r ∨ s ∧ r ∨ ∼ s ∵ p → q ≡ ∼ p ∨ q ≡ F ∨ T ∨ T ∨ F ∧ F ∨ T ≡ T ∨ T ∧ T ≡ T ∨ T ≡ T Option (d) do similar as option (a).
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

We have statements p,q→T and r,s→F
Option (a) q∧r∨∼p∧s≡T∧F∨F∧F
≡F∨F≡F
Option (b) ∼p→q↔r∧s≡p∨q→r∨s
∵p→q≡∼p∨q
≡∼pvq∨r∧s∧p∨q∨∼r∧s
∵p↔q≡∼p∨q∧pv-q
≡∼T∨T∨F∧F∧T∨F∨F∧F
≡F∨F∧T∨T≡F∧T≡F
Option (c) p→q∨r↔s
≡∼p∨q∨∼r∨s∧r∨∼s∵p→q≡∼p∨q
≡F∨T∨T∨F∧F∨T
≡T∨T∧T≡T∨T≡T
Option (d) do similar as option (a).

questions.solutionText: Solution n + 1 C n − 2 − n + 1 C n − 1 ≤ 50 n + 1 C n − 2 = n + 1 C 3 = ( n + 1 ) n ( n − 1 ) 6 n + 1 C n − 1 = n + 1 C 2 = ( n + 1 ) n 2 ( n + 1 ) n ( n − 1 ) 6 − ( n + 1 ) n 2 ≤ 50 ( n + 1 ) n 6 [ ( n − 1 ) − 3 ] ≤ 50 ( n + 1 ) n ( n − 4 ) 6 ≤ 50 n ( n + 1 ) ( n − 4 ) ≤ 300 For the expression to be valid: n ≥ 2 (for n + 1 C 3 to exist, n + 1 ≥ 3 ⇒ n ≥ 2 ). Also need n − 2 ≤ n + 1 and n − 1 ≤ n + 1 , which are always true. Possible positive integers: n = 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 , 6 , 7 , 8 ⇒ 7 values.
questions.solution: Solution n + 1 C n − 2 − n + 1 C n − 1 ≤ 50 n + 1 C n − 2 = n + 1 C 3 = ( n + 1 ) n ( n − 1 ) 6 n + 1 C n − 1 = n + 1 C 2 = ( n + 1 ) n 2 ( n + 1 ) n ( n − 1 ) 6 − ( n + 1 ) n 2 ≤ 50 ( n + 1 ) n 6 [ ( n − 1 ) − 3 ] ≤ 50 ( n + 1 ) n ( n − 4 ) 6 ≤ 50 n ( n + 1 ) ( n − 4 ) ≤ 300 For the expression to be valid: n ≥ 2 (for n + 1 C 3 to exist, n + 1 ≥ 3 ⇒ n ≥ 2 ). Also need n − 2 ≤ n + 1 and n − 1 ≤ n + 1 , which are always true. Possible positive integers: n = 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 , 6 , 7 , 8 ⇒ 7 values.

Solution
n+1Cn−2−n+1Cn−1≤50

n+1Cn−2=n+1C3=(n+1)n(n−1)6

n+1Cn−1=n+1C2=(n+1)n2

(n+1)n(n−1)6−(n+1)n2≤50

(n+1)n6[(n−1)−3]≤50

(n+1)n(n−4)6≤50

n(n+1)(n−4)≤300

For the expression to be valid: n≥2 (for n+1C3 to exist, n+1≥3⇒n≥2).

Also need n−2≤n+1 and n−1≤n+1, which are always true.

Possible positive integers: n=2,3,4,5,6,7,8 ⇒ 7 values.

questions.questionHtml: If x is rational and 4x2+1x2+16x+1x-57=0, then the product of all possible values of x is
questions.solutionText: Solution Given equation, 4 x 2 + 1 x 2 + 1 6 x + 1 x - 5 7 = 0 Let, x + 1 x = y ; x 2 + 1 x 2 = y 2 - 2 ⇒ 4 y 2 + 1 6 y - 6 5 = 0 ⇒ y = - 1 3 2 or ⁡ 5 2 When, y = 5 2 x + 1 x = 5 2 ⇒ x = 2 or 1 2 When, y = - 13 2 ⇒ x + 1 x = - 1 3 / 2 ⇒ 2 x 2 + 1 3 x + 2 = 0 ⇒ x = - 1 3 ± 1 5 3 4 Since x is rational, x = 2 or 1 2 Hence, their product is 1 .
questions.solution: Solution Given equation, 4 x 2 + 1 x 2 + 1 6 x + 1 x - 5 7 = 0 Let, x + 1 x = y ; x 2 + 1 x 2 = y 2 - 2 ⇒ 4 y 2 + 1 6 y - 6 5 = 0 ⇒ y = - 1 3 2 or ⁡ 5 2 When, y = 5 2 x + 1 x = 5 2 ⇒ x = 2 or 1 2 When, y = - 13 2 ⇒ x + 1 x = - 1 3 / 2 ⇒ 2 x 2 + 1 3 x + 2 = 0 ⇒ x = - 1 3 ± 1 5 3 4 Since x is rational, x = 2 or 1 2 Hence, their product is 1 .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Given equation,
4 x2+1x2+16x+1x-57=0

Let, x+1x=y; x2+1x2=y2-2

⇒ 4y2+16y-65=0

⇒ y=-132 or⁡ 52

When, y=52

x+1x=52⇒x=2 or 12

When, y=-132

⇒ x+1x=-13/2

⇒ 2x2+13x+2=0

⇒ x=-13±1534

Since x is rational, x=2 or 12

Hence, their product is 1.

questions.solutionText: Solution Given that, z 3 + i z - 1 = 0 Let one real root of given cubic equation is z Let z = α , α ∈ R ⇒ α 3 + i α - 1 = 0 ⇒ α 3 - 1 + i α = 0 + 0 i Comparing real and imaginary part, we get α 3 - 1 = 0 and α = 0 ⇒ α = 1 and α = 0 , which is not possible simultaneously. So, given equation has no real roots.

So, given equation has no real roots.

α3-1=0and α=0

So, given equation has no real roots.

questions.text: The sides of a right-angled triangle are in arithmetic progression. If the triangle has area 24 square units, then what is the length of its smallest side?
questions.questionText: The sides of a right-angled triangle are in arithmetic progression. If the triangle has area 24 square units, then what is the length of its smallest side?
questions.question: The sides of a right-angled triangle are in arithmetic progression. If the triangle has area 24 square units, then what is the length of its smallest side?
questions.prompt: The sides of a right-angled triangle are in arithmetic progression. If the triangle has area 24 square units, then what is the length of its smallest side?
questions.html: The sides of a right-angled triangle are in arithmetic progression. If the triangle has area 24 square units, then what is the length of its smallest side?
questions.questionHtml: The sides of a right-angled triangle are in arithmetic progression. If the triangle has area 24 square units, then what is the length of its smallest side?
questions.solutionText: Solution Let three sides of triangle be: a - d , a , a + d . Where, d > 0 , a > 0 . ⇒ length of smallest side = a - d units Now, ( a + d ) 2 = a 2 + ( a − d ) 2 ⇒ a a - 4 d = 0 ∴ a = 4 d .....(i) (As a = 0 is rejected) Also, 1 2 a . a - d = 24 ⇒ a a - d = 48 .....(ii) ∴ From (i) and (ii), we get a = 8 , d = 2 Hence, length of smallest side is a - d = 8 - 2 = 6 units
questions.solution: Solution Let three sides of triangle be: a - d , a , a + d . Where, d > 0 , a > 0 . ⇒ length of smallest side = a - d units Now, ( a + d ) 2 = a 2 + ( a − d ) 2 ⇒ a a - 4 d = 0 ∴ a = 4 d .....(i) (As a = 0 is rejected) Also, 1 2 a . a - d = 24 ⇒ a a - d = 48 .....(ii) ∴ From (i) and (ii), we get a = 8 , d = 2 Hence, length of smallest side is a - d = 8 - 2 = 6 units
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Let three sides of triangle be: a-d,a,a+d.

Where, d>0, a>0.

⇒ length of smallest side =a-d units

Now, (a+d)2=a2+(a−d)2

⇒ aa-4d=0

∴ a=4d .....(i)

(As a = 0 is rejected)

Also, 12a.a-d=24

⇒ aa-d=48 .....(ii)

∴ From (i) and (ii), we get a=8, d=2

Hence, length of smallest side is

a-d=8-2=6units

questions.text: If A 1 , A 2 ; G 1 , G 2 and H 1 , H 2 be two AM's, GM's and HM's between two quantities, then the value of G 1 G 2 H 1 H 2 is:

questions.questionHtml: If A1,A2; G1,G2 and H1,H2 be two AM's, GM's and HM's between two quantities, then the value of G1G2H1H2 is:
questions.solutionText: Solution Let the two quantities be a and b . A 1 + A 2 = a + b G 1 G 2 = a b H 1 = 3 a b a + 2 b , H 2 = 3 a b 2 a + b H 1 H 2 = 9 a 2 b 2 ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) H 1 + H 2 = 9 a b ( a + b ) ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) G 1 G 2 H 1 H 2 = a b ⋅ ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) 9 a 2 b 2 = ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) 9 a b A 1 + A 2 H 1 + H 2 = ( a + b ) ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) 9 a b ( a + b ) = ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) 9 a b ∴ G 1 G 2 H 1 H 2 = A 1 + A 2 H 1 + H 2
questions.solution: Solution Let the two quantities be a and b . A 1 + A 2 = a + b G 1 G 2 = a b H 1 = 3 a b a + 2 b , H 2 = 3 a b 2 a + b H 1 H 2 = 9 a 2 b 2 ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) H 1 + H 2 = 9 a b ( a + b ) ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) G 1 G 2 H 1 H 2 = a b ⋅ ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) 9 a 2 b 2 = ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) 9 a b A 1 + A 2 H 1 + H 2 = ( a + b ) ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) 9 a b ( a + b ) = ( a + 2 b ) ( 2 a + b ) 9 a b ∴ G 1 G 2 H 1 H 2 = A 1 + A 2 H 1 + H 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let the two quantities be a and b.

A1+A2=a+b

G1G2=ab

H1=3aba+2b, H2=3ab2a+b

H1H2=9a2b2(a+2b)(2a+b)

H1+H2=9ab(a+b)(a+2b)(2a+b)

G1G2H1H2=ab⋅(a+2b)(2a+b)9a2b2=(a+2b)(2a+b)9ab

A1+A2H1+H2=(a+b)(a+2b)(2a+b)9ab(a+b)=(a+2b)(2a+b)9ab

∴G1G2H1H2=A1+A2H1+H2

questions.questionText: The value of sec 2 ⁡ ( tan − 1 ⁡ 2 ) + cosec 2 ( cot − 1 ⁡ 3 ) is equal to
questions.question: The value of sec 2 ⁡ ( tan − 1 ⁡ 2 ) + cosec 2 ( cot − 1 ⁡ 3 ) is equal to
questions.prompt: The value of sec 2 ⁡ ( tan − 1 ⁡ 2 ) + cosec 2 ( cot − 1 ⁡ 3 ) is equal to
questions.html: The value of sec2⁡(tan−1⁡2)+cosec2(cot−1⁡3) is equal to
questions.questionHtml: The value of sec2⁡(tan−1⁡2)+cosec2(cot−1⁡3) is equal to
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
sec2⁡(tan−1⁡2)+cosec2(cot−1⁡3)={1+tan2⁡(tan−1⁡2)}+{1+cot2⁡(cot−1⁡3)}
=1+{tan⁡(tan−1⁡2)}2+1+{cot⁡(cot−1⁡3)}2
=1+22+1+3=15

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Given the points A ( x = 2 , y = 3 , z = − 1 ) and B ( r = 4 , θ = 30 ∘ , φ = 120 ∘ ) . Find distance between A and B
questions.questionText: Given the points A ( x = 2 , y = 3 , z = − 1 ) and B ( r = 4 , θ = 30 ∘ , φ = 120 ∘ ) . Find distance between A and B
questions.question: Given the points A ( x = 2 , y = 3 , z = − 1 ) and B ( r = 4 , θ = 30 ∘ , φ = 120 ∘ ) . Find distance between A and B
questions.prompt: Given the points A ( x = 2 , y = 3 , z = − 1 ) and B ( r = 4 , θ = 30 ∘ , φ = 120 ∘ ) . Find distance between A and B
questions.html: Given the points A(x=2,y=3,z=−1) and B(r=4,θ=30∘,φ=120∘). Find distance between A and B
questions.questionHtml: Given the points A(x=2,y=3,z=−1) and B(r=4,θ=30∘,φ=120∘). Find distance between A and B
questions.solutionText: Solution The conversion from spherical coordinates ( r , θ , φ ) to cartesian coordinates ( x , y , z ) is x = r cos ⁡ φ sin ⁡ θ y = r sin ⁡ φ sin ⁡ θ z = r cos ⁡ θ A ( x = 2 , y = 3 , z = − 1 ) and B ( r = 4 , θ = 30 ∘ , φ = 120 ∘ ) Point A is given in cartesian coordinates and Point B is given in spherical coordinates. By converting the given spherical coordinates to cartesian coordinates, x = 4 × cos ⁡ 120 sin ⁡ 30 = − 1 y = 4 × 3 2 × 1 2 = 3 z = 4 × 3 2 = 2 3 A ( 2 , 3 , − 1 ) , B ( − 1 , 3 , 2 3 ) The distance between the points A and B is, A B = 3 2 + ( 3 − 3 ) 2 + ( − 1 − 2 3 ) 2 = 5.53
questions.solution: Solution The conversion from spherical coordinates ( r , θ , φ ) to cartesian coordinates ( x , y , z ) is x = r cos ⁡ φ sin ⁡ θ y = r sin ⁡ φ sin ⁡ θ z = r cos ⁡ θ A ( x = 2 , y = 3 , z = − 1 ) and B ( r = 4 , θ = 30 ∘ , φ = 120 ∘ ) Point A is given in cartesian coordinates and Point B is given in spherical coordinates. By converting the given spherical coordinates to cartesian coordinates, x = 4 × cos ⁡ 120 sin ⁡ 30 = − 1 y = 4 × 3 2 × 1 2 = 3 z = 4 × 3 2 = 2 3 A ( 2 , 3 , − 1 ) , B ( − 1 , 3 , 2 3 ) The distance between the points A and B is, A B = 3 2 + ( 3 − 3 ) 2 + ( − 1 − 2 3 ) 2 = 5.53
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The conversion from spherical coordinates (r,θ,φ) to cartesian coordinates (x,y,z) is
x=rcos⁡φsin⁡θy=rsin⁡φsin⁡θz=rcos⁡θ
A(x=2,y=3,z=−1) and B(r=4,θ=30∘,φ=120∘)
Point A is given in cartesian coordinates and Point B is given in spherical coordinates.
By converting the given spherical coordinates to cartesian coordinates,
x=4×cos⁡120sin⁡30=−1y=4×32×12=3z=4×32=23 A(2,3,−1),B(−1,3,23)
The distance between the points A and B is,
AB=32+(3−3)2+(−1−23)2=5.53

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The solution of the differential equation d y d x = y x + ϕ y x ϕ ′ y x is:
questions.questionText: The solution of the differential equation d y d x = y x + ϕ y x ϕ ′ y x is:
questions.question: The solution of the differential equation d y d x = y x + ϕ y x ϕ ′ y x is:
questions.prompt: The solution of the differential equation d y d x = y x + ϕ y x ϕ ′ y x is:
questions.html: The solution of the differential equation dydx=yx+ϕ yxϕ′yx is:
questions.questionHtml: The solution of the differential equation dydx=yx+ϕ yxϕ′yx is:
questions.solutionText: Solution Given equation is: d y d x = y x + ϕ y x ϕ ′ y x . . . . . . ( i ) Put y = v x ⇒ d y d x = v + x d v d x Now, equation i becomes: v + x d v d x = v + ϕ ( v ) ϕ ′ ( v ) ⇒ ϕ ′ ( v ) ϕ ( v ) d v = d x x On integrating both sides, we get: ∫ ϕ ′ ( v ) ϕ ( v ) d v = ∫ 1 x d x ⇒ log ⁡ ϕ v = log ⁡ x + log ⁡ k ⇒ log ⁡ ϕ v = log ⁡ x k ⇒ ϕ v = k x ⇒ ϕ y x = k x
questions.solution: Solution Given equation is: d y d x = y x + ϕ y x ϕ ′ y x . . . . . . ( i ) Put y = v x ⇒ d y d x = v + x d v d x Now, equation i becomes: v + x d v d x = v + ϕ ( v ) ϕ ′ ( v ) ⇒ ϕ ′ ( v ) ϕ ( v ) d v = d x x On integrating both sides, we get: ∫ ϕ ′ ( v ) ϕ ( v ) d v = ∫ 1 x d x ⇒ log ⁡ ϕ v = log ⁡ x + log ⁡ k ⇒ log ⁡ ϕ v = log ⁡ x k ⇒ ϕ v = k x ⇒ ϕ y x = k x
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Given equation is:

dydx=yx+ϕ yxϕ′yx......(i)
Put y=vx ⇒ dydx=v+xdvdx
Now, equation i becomes:
v+xdvdx=v+ϕ(v)ϕ′(v)
⇒ ϕ′(v)ϕ(v)dv=dxx
On integrating both sides, we get:
∫ϕ′(v)ϕ(v)dv=∫1xdx
⇒log⁡ϕv=log⁡x+log⁡k
⇒log⁡ϕv=log⁡xk
⇒ ϕv=kx ⇒ ϕ yx=kx

questions.solutionText: Solution f ( x ) = [ x ] sin ⁡ ( π x ) LHD = lim h → 0 f ( k − h ) − f ( k ) − h = lim h → 0 [ k − h ] sin ⁡ π ( k − h ) − [ k ] sin ⁡ k π − h = lim h → 0 ( k − 1 ) sin ⁡ ( k π − π h ) − k sin ⁡ k π − h = lim h → 0 ( − 1 ) k + 1 ( k − 1 ) sinh ⁡ π − 0 − h … [ ∵ k ∈ I ] = ( − 1 ) k ( k − 1 ) π
questions.solution: Solution f ( x ) = [ x ] sin ⁡ ( π x ) LHD = lim h → 0 f ( k − h ) − f ( k ) − h = lim h → 0 [ k − h ] sin ⁡ π ( k − h ) − [ k ] sin ⁡ k π − h = lim h → 0 ( k − 1 ) sin ⁡ ( k π − π h ) − k sin ⁡ k π − h = lim h → 0 ( − 1 ) k + 1 ( k − 1 ) sinh ⁡ π − 0 − h … [ ∵ k ∈ I ] = ( − 1 ) k ( k − 1 ) π
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
f(x)=[x]sin⁡(πx) LHD =limh→0f(k−h)−f(k)−h=limh→0[k−h]sin⁡π(k−h)−[k]sin⁡kπ−h=limh→0(k−1)sin⁡(kπ−πh)−ksin⁡kπ−h=limh→0(−1)k+1(k−1)sinh⁡π−0−h…[∵k∈I]=(−1)k(k−1)π

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Set A has 3 elements and set B has 4 elements. The number of injection that can be defined from A to B is
questions.questionText: Set A has 3 elements and set B has 4 elements. The number of injection that can be defined from A to B is
questions.question: Set A has 3 elements and set B has 4 elements. The number of injection that can be defined from A to B is
questions.prompt: Set A has 3 elements and set B has 4 elements. The number of injection that can be defined from A to B is
questions.html: Set A has 3 elements and set B has 4 elements. The number of injection that can be defined from A to B is
questions.questionHtml: Set A has 3 elements and set B has 4 elements. The number of injection that can be defined from A to B is
questions.solutionText: Solution The total number of injective functions from a set A containing 3 elements to a set B containing 4 elements is equal to the total number of arrangements of 4 by taking 3 at a time i.e., 4 P 3 = 24 .
questions.solution: Solution The total number of injective functions from a set A containing 3 elements to a set B containing 4 elements is equal to the total number of arrangements of 4 by taking 3 at a time i.e., 4 P 3 = 24 .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The total number of injective functions from a set A containing 3 elements to a set B containing 4 elements is equal to the total number of arrangements of 4 by taking 3 at a time i.e., 4P3=24.

questions.solutionText: Solution Coefficient of x k 1 y k 2 z k 3 , in the expansion of x + y + z n is n ! k 1 ! k 2 ! k 3 ! , where k 1 + k 2 + k 3 = n Hence, coefficient of a 8 b c = 10 ! 8 ! 1 ! 1 ! = 90 .

questions.solutionText: Solution We have, x d y d x 3 2 = 2 d 2 y d x 2 + sin x + 1 Square both the sides we get, x 2 d y d x 3 = 2 d 2 y d x + sin x + 1 2 Here, Order = p = 2 Degree = q = 2 Then, p q + q p = 2 2 + 2 2 = 8

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

We have, xdydx32=2d2ydx2+sinx+1

Square both the sides we get,

x2dydx3=2d2ydx+sinx+12

Here, Order=p=2

Degree=q=2

Then, pq+qp=22+22=8

questions.text: If f x = sin x , x < 0 cos x - x - 1 , x ≥ 0 , then g x = f x is non-differentiable for
questions.questionText: If f x = sin x , x < 0 cos x - x - 1 , x ≥ 0 , then g x = f x is non-differentiable for
questions.question: If f x = sin x , x < 0 cos x - x - 1 , x ≥ 0 , then g x = f x is non-differentiable for
questions.prompt: If f x = sin x , x < 0 cos x - x - 1 , x ≥ 0 , then g x = f x is non-differentiable for
questions.html: If fx=sinx,x<0cosx-x-1,x≥0, then gx=fx is non-differentiable for
questions.questionHtml: If fx=sinx,x<0cosx-x-1,x≥0, then gx=fx is non-differentiable for
questions.solutionText: Solution f x = sin x , x < 0 cos x - x - 1 , x ≥ 0 ⇒ f x = cos x - x - 1 , x ∈ R (as x < 0 is not possible and x ≥ 0 is true ∀ x ∈ R ) This is non-differentiable at x = 0 and when x - 1 = 0 ⇒ x = ± 1 Hence, f x has exactly three points of non-differentiability.
questions.solution: Solution f x = sin x , x < 0 cos x - x - 1 , x ≥ 0 ⇒ f x = cos x - x - 1 , x ∈ R (as x < 0 is not possible and x ≥ 0 is true ∀ x ∈ R ) This is non-differentiable at x = 0 and when x - 1 = 0 ⇒ x = ± 1 Hence, f x has exactly three points of non-differentiability.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

fx=sinx,x<0cosx-x-1,x≥0

⇒fx=cosx-x-1, x∈R

(as x<0 is not possible and x≥0 is true ∀ x∈R)

This is non-differentiable at x=0 and when x-1=0⇒x=±1

Hence, fx has exactly three points of non-differentiability.

questions.text: The total number of tangents through the points 3 , 5 that can be drawn to the ellipses 3 x 2 + 5 y 2 = 32 and 25 x 2 + 9 y 2 = 450 is
questions.questionText: The total number of tangents through the points 3 , 5 that can be drawn to the ellipses 3 x 2 + 5 y 2 = 32 and 25 x 2 + 9 y 2 = 450 is

questions.html: The total number of tangents through the points 3,5 that can be drawn to the ellipses 3x2+5y2=32 and 25x2+9y2=450 is
questions.questionHtml: The total number of tangents through the points 3,5 that can be drawn to the ellipses 3x2+5y2=32 and 25x2+9y2=450 is
questions.solutionText: Solution Let S 1 = 3 x 2 + 5 y 2 - 32 and S 2 = 25 x 2 + 9 y 2 - 450 For the point 3 , 5 S 1 = 3 3 2 + 5 5 2 - 32 = 120 > 0 and S 2 = 25 3 2 + 9 5 2 - 450 = 225 + 225 - 450 = 0 ∴ Point 3 , 5 lies outside the first ellipse and for the second ellipse lies on the ellipse. Hence, two tangents for first ellipse and one tangent for the second ellipse can be drawn So total number of tangents are = 3
questions.solution: Solution Let S 1 = 3 x 2 + 5 y 2 - 32 and S 2 = 25 x 2 + 9 y 2 - 450 For the point 3 , 5 S 1 = 3 3 2 + 5 5 2 - 32 = 120 > 0 and S 2 = 25 3 2 + 9 5 2 - 450 = 225 + 225 - 450 = 0 ∴ Point 3 , 5 lies outside the first ellipse and for the second ellipse lies on the ellipse. Hence, two tangents for first ellipse and one tangent for the second ellipse can be drawn So total number of tangents are = 3
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let S1=3x2+5y2-32

and S2=25x2+9y2-450

For the point 3,5
S1=332+552-32=120>0
and S2=2532+952-450
=225+225-450=0
∴ Point 3, 5 lies outside the first ellipse and for the second ellipse lies on the ellipse.
Hence, two tangents for first ellipse and one tangent for the second ellipse can be drawn

So total number of tangents are =3

questions.text: For two events A and B if P A = P A B = 1 4 and P B A = 1 2 , then
questions.questionText: For two events A and B if P A = P A B = 1 4 and P B A = 1 2 , then
questions.question: For two events A and B if P A = P A B = 1 4 and P B A = 1 2 , then
questions.prompt: For two events A and B if P A = P A B = 1 4 and P B A = 1 2 , then
questions.html: For two events A and B if PA=PAB=14 and PBA=12, then
questions.questionHtml: For two events A and B if PA=PAB=14 and PBA=12, then
questions.solutionText: Solution Given, P B A = 1 2 ⇒ P B ∩ A P A = 1 2 ⇒ P B ∩ A = 1 2 × 1 4 = 1 8 And, P A B = 1 4 ⇒ P A ∩ B P B = 1 4 ⇒ P B = 4 P A ∩ B ⇒ P B = 1 2 ∴ P A ∩ B = 1 8 = 1 2 · 1 4 = P A · P B So, events A and B are independent. Now, P A ' B = P A ' ∩ B P ( B ) ⇒ P A ' B = P A ' P B P B = 3 4 And, P B ' A ' = P B ∩ A ' P A ' ⇒ P B ' A ' = P B ' P A ' P A ' = 1 2
questions.solution: Solution Given, P B A = 1 2 ⇒ P B ∩ A P A = 1 2 ⇒ P B ∩ A = 1 2 × 1 4 = 1 8 And, P A B = 1 4 ⇒ P A ∩ B P B = 1 4 ⇒ P B = 4 P A ∩ B ⇒ P B = 1 2 ∴ P A ∩ B = 1 8 = 1 2 · 1 4 = P A · P B So, events A and B are independent. Now, P A ' B = P A ' ∩ B P ( B ) ⇒ P A ' B = P A ' P B P B = 3 4 And, P B ' A ' = P B ∩ A ' P A ' ⇒ P B ' A ' = P B ' P A ' P A ' = 1 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Given,

PBA=12

⇒PB∩APA=12

⇒ PB∩A=12×14=18

And,

PAB=14

⇒PA∩BPB=14

⇒ PB=4PA∩B

⇒ PB=12

∴ PA∩B=18=12·14=PA·PB

So, events A and B are independent.

Now,

PA'B=PA'∩BP(B)

⇒ PA'B=PA'PBPB=34

And,

PB'A'=PB∩A'PA'

⇒ PB'A'=PB'PA'PA'=12

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If tan − 1 ⁡ x + tan − 1 ⁡ y + tan − 1 ⁡ z = π 2 , then which of the following relations must be true?
questions.questionText: If tan − 1 ⁡ x + tan − 1 ⁡ y + tan − 1 ⁡ z = π 2 , then which of the following relations must be true?
questions.question: If tan − 1 ⁡ x + tan − 1 ⁡ y + tan − 1 ⁡ z = π 2 , then which of the following relations must be true?
questions.prompt: If tan − 1 ⁡ x + tan − 1 ⁡ y + tan − 1 ⁡ z = π 2 , then which of the following relations must be true?
questions.html: If tan−1⁡x+tan−1⁡y+tan−1⁡z=π2, then which of the following relations must be true?
questions.questionHtml: If tan−1⁡x+tan−1⁡y+tan−1⁡z=π2, then which of the following relations must be true?
questions.solutionText: Solution tan − 1 ⁡ x + tan − 1 ⁡ y + tan − 1 ⁡ z = π 2 Let tan − 1 ⁡ x + tan − 1 ⁡ y = α , so α + tan − 1 ⁡ z = π 2 α = π 2 − tan − 1 ⁡ z = cot − 1 ⁡ z = tan − 1 ⁡ 1 z tan ⁡ α = 1 z Also, tan ⁡ α = x + y 1 − x y x + y 1 − x y = 1 z z ( x + y ) = 1 − x y x z + y z = 1 − x y x y + y z + z x = 1
questions.solution: Solution tan − 1 ⁡ x + tan − 1 ⁡ y + tan − 1 ⁡ z = π 2 Let tan − 1 ⁡ x + tan − 1 ⁡ y = α , so α + tan − 1 ⁡ z = π 2 α = π 2 − tan − 1 ⁡ z = cot − 1 ⁡ z = tan − 1 ⁡ 1 z tan ⁡ α = 1 z Also, tan ⁡ α = x + y 1 − x y x + y 1 − x y = 1 z z ( x + y ) = 1 − x y x z + y z = 1 − x y x y + y z + z x = 1
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
tan−1⁡x+tan−1⁡y+tan−1⁡z=π2

Let tan−1⁡x+tan−1⁡y=α, so α+tan−1⁡z=π2

α=π2−tan−1⁡z=cot−1⁡z=tan−1⁡1z

tan⁡α=1z

Also, tan⁡α=x+y1−xy

x+y1−xy=1z

z(x+y)=1−xy

xz+yz=1−xy

xy+yz+zx=1

questions.html: If there are 140 students numbered from 1 to 140; out of which those divisible by 2 took maths, those divisible by 3 took physics and those divisible by 5 took chemistry. Then the number of candidates those didn't take any subject
questions.questionHtml: If there are 140 students numbered from 1 to 140; out of which those divisible by 2 took maths, those divisible by 3 took physics and those divisible by 5 took chemistry. Then the number of candidates those didn't take any subject
questions.solutionText: Solution n P ≡ 140 3 = 46 n C ≡ 140 5 = 28 n M ≡ 140 2 = 70 n P ∪​ C ∪​ M = n P + n C + n M - n P ∩​​ C - n C ∩​ M - n M ∩​ P + n P ∩​ M ∩​ C n P ∪​ C ∪​ M = 46 + 28 + 70 - 140 15 - 140 10 - 140 6 + 140 30 = 144 - 9 - 14 - 23 + 4 = 102 Hence, number of students who did not take any subject& = 140 - 102 = 38 .

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

nP≡1403=46
nC≡1405=28
nM≡1402=70


n P∪​C∪​M=nP+nC+nM-nP ∩​​C- nC∩​M- nM∩​P+ nP∩​M∩​C
n P∪​C∪​M=46+28+70-14015-14010-1406+14030
=144-9-14-23+4
=102

Hence, number of students who did not take any subject& =140-102=38.

questions.solutionText: Solution The curve has two loops is symmetrical about the x - axis and y - axis. Required area is A = 4 ∫ 0 a x a a 2 - x 2 d x Let a 2 - x 2 = t 2 ⇒ x d x = - t d t A = - 4 a ∫ a 0 t 2 d t = 4 a ∫ 0 a t 2 d t = 4 3 a t 3 0 a = 4 3 a 2 sq. units

questions.questionText: If f ( x ) = x n − n , then the value of f ( − 1 ) + f ′ ( − 1 ) 1 ! ( 2 ) + f ″ ( − 1 ) 2 ! ( 2 2 ) + ⋯ + f n ( − 1 ) n ! ( 2 n ) is:
questions.question: If f ( x ) = x n − n , then the value of f ( − 1 ) + f ′ ( − 1 ) 1 ! ( 2 ) + f ″ ( − 1 ) 2 ! ( 2 2 ) + ⋯ + f n ( − 1 ) n ! ( 2 n ) is:
questions.prompt: If f ( x ) = x n − n , then the value of f ( − 1 ) + f ′ ( − 1 ) 1 ! ( 2 ) + f ″ ( − 1 ) 2 ! ( 2 2 ) + ⋯ + f n ( − 1 ) n ! ( 2 n ) is:
questions.html: If f(x)=xn−n, then the value of f(−1)+f′(−1)1!(2)+f″(−1)2!(22)+⋯+fn(−1)n!(2n) is:
questions.questionHtml: If f(x)=xn−n, then the value of f(−1)+f′(−1)1!(2)+f″(−1)2!(22)+⋯+fn(−1)n!(2n) is:
questions.solutionText: Solution The expression resembles the Taylor series expansion of f ( x ) centered at x = a : f ( x ) = f ( a ) + f ′ ( a ) 1 ! ( x − a ) + f ″ ( a ) 2 ! ( x − a ) 2 + ⋯ + f ( n ) ( a ) n ! ( x − a ) n Comparing with the given expression, the center point is a = − 1 . The ( x − a ) k portion corresponds to 2 k , so: ( x − a ) = 2 ⇒ x − ( − 1 ) = 2 ⇒ x = 1 Since a polynomial of degree n is exactly equal to its n -th degree Taylor expansion, the entire expression is simply f ( 1 ) . f ( 1 ) = ( 1 ) n − n = 1 − n
questions.solution: Solution The expression resembles the Taylor series expansion of f ( x ) centered at x = a : f ( x ) = f ( a ) + f ′ ( a ) 1 ! ( x − a ) + f ″ ( a ) 2 ! ( x − a ) 2 + ⋯ + f ( n ) ( a ) n ! ( x − a ) n Comparing with the given expression, the center point is a = − 1 . The ( x − a ) k portion corresponds to 2 k , so: ( x − a ) = 2 ⇒ x − ( − 1 ) = 2 ⇒ x = 1 Since a polynomial of degree n is exactly equal to its n -th degree Taylor expansion, the entire expression is simply f ( 1 ) . f ( 1 ) = ( 1 ) n − n = 1 − n
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The expression resembles the Taylor series expansion of f(x) centered at x=a:

f(x)=f(a)+f′(a)1!(x−a)+f″(a)2!(x−a)2+⋯+f(n)(a)n!(x−a)n

Comparing with the given expression, the center point is a=−1.

The (x−a)k portion corresponds to 2k, so:

(x−a)=2⇒x−(−1)=2⇒x=1

Since a polynomial of degree n is exactly equal to its n-th degree Taylor expansion, the entire expression is simply f(1).

f(1)=(1)n−n=1−n
